title: An interactive system for 3D spatial relationship query by integrating tree-based element indexing and LLM-based agent
contribution: 提出基于openBIM标准的几何信息提取算法、两级空间索引结构和LLM多智能体协作框架，实现BIM三维空间关系的自然语言交互式查询，查询效率提升70%，查询理解准确率达92.1%
abstract: This paper develops an interactive natural language spatial query system for BIM elements based on an LLM-based multi-agent framework, addressing three challenges: error-prone extraction of complex spatial geometric information, low efficiency in processing large numbers of elements, and inadequate integration of LLM with BIM spatial query tasks. The system features three contributions: (1) an openBIM standards-based algorithm to extract complex geometric information from IFC files; (2) a two-level spatial index (adjacency graph for spaces plus AABB tree for elements) to improve search efficiency; (3) an LLM-based multi-agent collaboration framework with four specialized agents to decompose and execute spatial query tasks. Validated on three BIM models, the system achieves 92.1% correctness in query understanding and reduces average total query time by 70%.
methodology: The methodology consists of three steps: (1) IFC geometric information extraction based on openBIM MVD standards, using IfcOpenShell and FreeCAD to parse IFC files and convert various geometric representations into triangulated B-rep; (2) a two-level spatial index that first indexes building spaces using an adjacency graph (1st level) and then indexes building elements within each space using AABB trees (2nd level); (3) an LLM-based multi-agent interactive query system built with AutoGen Studio, comprising four GPT-4 agents (Group_chat_manage, Understand_assistant, Spatial_index_assistant, Calculate_assistant) with a bSDD-aided prompt strategy for query understanding and embedded Python skills for spatial relationship calculation using the 9-Intersection Model.
results: The system successfully extracts geometric information from three complex BIM models (CYT Building with 7,718 elements, Shaw Auditorium with 34,214 elements, Shopping Mall with 23,383 elements). The two-level spatial index reduces average query time by over 70% compared to brute-force search without indexing. The bSDD-aided prompt achieves 92.1% correctness in query understanding, outperforming common prompts by approximately 20%. GPT-4 shows the highest understanding capability among tested LLMs. The multi-agent framework successfully decomposes spatial query tasks and provides user-friendly natural language answers.
